commands.review_summary:
The branch makes ChatUsageFormatter use invariant numeric formatting, updates Windows known-folder tests to compare OS-resolved paths, serializes AppVersionInfo override tests, tightens one request-id assertion, and marks test projects explicitly.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main formats compact token counts with current-culture numeric formatting and tests known folders by English suffix. I did not independently run a localized Windows host, but the PR body supplies Portuguese Windows validation for the changed paths.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers should run or confirm the AGENTS-required full build, Shared tests, and Tray tests before merge.

target.title: Fix locale-sensitive Windows tests and technical token formatting
target.body:
Tracking issue: #784

## Summary

This PR makes a small set of Windows test/formatting paths safe on localized Windows installations:

- Compare sandbox Documents, Desktop, and Downloads grants against OS-provided known-folder paths instead of English folder suffixes.
- Format compact technical token counts with `InvariantCulture`, so regional decimal separators do not change test-facing output.
- Serialize tests that mutate `AppVersionInfo.TestOverride`, including `WindowsNodeClientTests`, to avoid parallel shared-state races.

This is intended as PR1 in the Windows 25H2 / MXC validation tracking issue. It is independent from the MXC runtime enablement PR and can be reviewed separately.

## Why

On a Portuguese Windows setup, known folders can resolve to localized or redirected locations such as `Documentos` and `Ambiente de Trabalho`. The previous tests assumed English path suffixes like `Documents`, `Desktop`, or `Downloads`, which makes otherwise correct behavior look broken on localized Windows.

The compact token count formatter also used current-culture numeric formatting. That can produce comma decimals such as `15,3K` in locales where the technical/test contract expects stable dot-decimal output.

## Changed Files

- `src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/Chat/ChatUsageFormatter.cs`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/AppVersionInfoTestCollection.cs`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/AppVersionInfoTests.cs`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/Mxc/MxcPolicyBuilderTests.cs`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/WindowsNodeClientTests.cs`

## Validation

Local validation on Windows Release Preview 25H2 build `26200.8728`, Portuguese Windows locale:

- `./build.ps1`: passed, all builds succeeded.
- Focused `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` filter `MxcPolicyBuilderTests|AppVersionInfoTests|WindowsNodeClientTests`: passed, `82` total, `0` failed.
- Focused `OpenClaw.Tray.Tests` filter `ChatUsageFormatterTests`: passed, `9` total, `0` failed.
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Notes

This PR intentionally does not change MXC availability, `system.run`, Gateway pairing, WSL Gateway setup, release versioning, or installed Companion behavior.

Broad-suite note: this local split validation does not claim the whole suite is green. Broad runs in this environment hit existing `%APPDATA%`/ACL/`HttpListener` environment failures outside this PR's changed surfaces.

Follow-up note: full Gateway-mode and installed Companion validation are intentionally out of scope for this PR and are planned for a later PR after the bounded MXC validation PR.
